name: Pocket door
description: An interior door that slides horizontally into a hidden cavity within the adjacent wall rather than swinging on hinges, leaving zero swing arc and reclaiming the floor space a conventional door would sterilize. The doorway reads as a single flush opening flanked by a thicker partition that conceals the parked leaf; a slim track at the head and a recessed pull at the leading edge are the only outward cues. Particularly suited to tight rooms, en-suite bathrooms, and walk-in closets, and to formal arrangements where two parlors are linked by a wide bay that should disappear completely when open. Historically common in Victorian double-parlor plans and reborn in modern minimalist interiors. Single-leaf for narrow openings, double-leaf converging from both jambs for wide ceremonial bays.
